texto: It never occurs to her that she will not be a writer and only occasionally does it occur to her, depressingly, that she is going to grow into a woman, not a man.
explicacao: Citação de Jean Stafford sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “It never occurs to her that she will not be a writer and only occasionally does it occur to her, …” articula desenvolvimento-pessoal, ambition, internalized-sexism em registro de desenvolvimento pessoal, com relevância para contextos como internalized-sexism.

frase_original: It never occurs to her that she will not be a writer and only occasionally does it occur to her, depressingly, that she is going to grow into a woman, not a man.

texto: To her own heart, which was shaped exactly like a valentine, there came a winglike palpitation, a delicate exigency, and all the fragrance of all the flowery springtime love affairs that ever were seemed waiting for them in the whisky bottle. To mingle their pain their handshake had promised them, was to produce a separate entity, like a child that could shift for itself, and they scrambled hastily toward this profound and pastoral experience.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jean Stafford: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “To her own heart, which was shaped exactly like a valentine, there came a winglike palpitation, a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, love, lovers pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de lovers e whiskey.

frase_original: To her own heart, which was shaped exactly like a valentine, there came a winglike palpitation, a delicate exigency, and all the fragrance of all the flowery springtime love affairs that ever were seemed waiting for them in the whisky bottle. To mingle their pain their handshake had promised them, was to produce a separate entity, like a child that could shift for itself, and they scrambled hastily toward this profound and pastoral experience.
